title: Pungent Ethiopian Hangover-cure Ye-Beg-Alicha
description: A traditional Ethiopian stew that is said to cure hangovers, made with the spicy 'berbere' spice blend and incorporating tukbaegi earthenware cookware for added depth of flavor. This hearty meal is perfect for a weekend brunch or a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine the berbere spice blend, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, allspice, salt, black pepper, and red pepper. Mix well to combine.
Heat 1 tablespoon of niter kibbeh in a tukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they are lightly browned and caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken thighs to the pot and cook until they are browned on all sides and cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set it aside on a plate.
In the same pot, add the beef broth, heavy whipping cream, and crushed red pepper.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it has thickened slightly, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the niter kibbeh and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken back to the pot and stir in the chopped fresh mint.
Season the stew with salt to taste and serve hot, garnished with scallions and fresh cilantro.
Serve the stew over rice or with injera bread for a traditional Ethiopian meal.
